items.summary: Details SOLIDWORKS 2026 AI features now reaching general availability in July 2026: AI drawing generation, the LEO virtual companion, assembly-hardware recognition, and the DraftSight AI Agent for CAD standards verification across thousands of drawings. Matters because these features represent a production-ready CAD copilot layer integrated directly into Dassault's mainstream modeling environment.

items.title: Cadence, NVIDIA Bring Autonomous Virtual Engineer for Chip Design
items.summary: Cadence extends ChipStack to Level-5 autonomy with NVIDIA Nemotron models, enabling AI agents to orchestrate hundreds of dynamic RTL simulations and reduce a typical five-week verification loop to under one day. Matters because this is the first fully autonomous EDA agent covering chip design and verification end-to-end, representing a threshold moment for AI in semiconductor design.

items.title: Bringing Fusion onto Claude for Creative Work
items.summary: Autodesk's official technical explainer on the Claude for Creative Work + Fusion MCP integration: Claude can read geometry, modify parts, run Fusion API operations, and write parametric history directly into active CAD files using natural language prompts. Matters because it details how a major LLM's tool-use capability is being wired into a professional CAD environment at the API level, setting a pattern for AI-in-CAD architecture.

items.title: Autodesk Announces Updates to AI Expansion Across Design & Manufacturing Portfolio
items.summary: Autodesk rolls Autodesk Assistant out across its full Product Design & Manufacturing portfolio—Fusion, Inventor, Moldflow, and more—with prompt-to-API capabilities in Fusion giving AI agents access to nearly all modeling operations and a new Design & Make Marketplace for certified MCP connectors. Matters because it signals Autodesk's commitment to agentic AI across its entire CAD/CAM/CAE stack, not just individual products.

items.title: SimScale Publishes 2026 State of Engineering AI Report
items.summary: SimScale's 2026 industry survey finds engineering teams using AI-enabled simulation workflows generate nearly 4x more design variants per program than conventional teams, with autonomous simulation orchestration moving from experimental pilots to routine use. Matters because it provides empirical evidence that AI-driven simulation is genuinely accelerating exploration across the design space, validating the surrogate-model and physics-aware-surrogate investment thesis.

items.title: Siemens Updates Designcenter Solid Edge 2026 with AI and Cloud-Driven Enhancements
items.summary: Designcenter Solid Edge 2026 debuts Design Copilot (conversational AI for in-environment help), Magnetic Snap Assembly (AI-driven constraint automation), and automatic 2D drawing generation covering 80% of views with minimal input, all shipping as part of the Siemens Xcelerator portfolio. Matters because it shows Siemens matching Dassault and Autodesk in embedding generative AI directly into mainstream mechanical CAD workflows.

items.title: Cadence Launches ChipStack AI Super Agent for Chip Design and Verification
items.summary: Cadence unveils ChipStack, the first agentic AI workflow for front-end silicon design and verification, delivering up to 10X productivity gains by autonomously coding RTL, generating testbenches, orchestrating regression runs, and debugging failures. Matters because it establishes a concrete benchmark for what agentic AI can do in EDA today, before the Level-5 autonomous engineer announcement three months later.

items.title: Figma Acquires Team Behind a Vibe-Coding App
items.summary: Figma acquires Bud (formerly Orchids), a vibe-coding and AI agent platform, to deepen its bet on AI-assisted app building alongside Figma Make, Codex, and Claude Code integrations. Signals Figma's pivot from a static design canvas toward an integrated AI agent ecosystem for prototyping and production.

items.title: DESIGN.md Examples for AI Agents — Refero Styles
items.summary: Refero Styles offers 2,000+ AI-readable DESIGN.md files distilling real product websites' visual systems (colors, typography, spacing, components) for direct consumption by coding agents like Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex. Formalizes design systems as machine-readable context that agents can act on without custom tooling.

items.title: How Decagon Uses AI for Design System Saturation
items.summary: Decagon built the Deco design system using Figma MCP and Figma Make to keep design and code synchronized as AI agents shipped features at high velocity, cutting revision cycles and enabling rapid customer-driven prototyping. Demonstrates a live MCP-powered feedback loop between designers and coding agents at a fast-scaling AI company.

items.title: How I Polish Software That Agents Built
items.summary: Engineering teams now receive overnight agent-built pull requests ready to merge, leaving the human's core job as judging whether the result is "any good" and pushing it further through taste and craft. Frames the final quality pass — sitting with the product and deciding what feels right — as the irreducible human contribution in agentic software development.

items.title: Craft Still Matters, But It's About Outcomes
items.summary: As AI commoditizes design production, designer value shifts from pixel-perfect artifacts to judgment: choosing the right problem, validating with real users, and setting quality standards for AI-generated outputs. Success requires making tacit expertise explicit through reusable context, scaffolded agents, and clear evaluation principles.

items.title: Claude Code on Desktop Now Has an In-App Browser
items.summary: Claude Code's desktop app gained a built-in sandboxed browser that lets the agent pull up docs, designs, and websites while iterating on local dev servers — closing the perception-execution loop for designers and developers. The browser is configurable for session persistence and protected by safety classifiers for external site interactions.

items.title: GPT-5.6 is Now Available in Figma Make
items.summary: Figma added OpenAI's GPT-5.6 to Figma Make, improving quality and speed of AI-generated prototypes from prompts or existing designs — including self-healing errors, layout adaptation, and fidelity to source files. The addition positions Figma Make as the primary design-to-code AI surface, giving designers a more capable model to prompt directly inside their workflow.

items.title: We Didn't Teach Our Designers AI: We Built a Place Where They Could Learn It Together
items.summary: Slack's design team ran a company-wide Builder Days event and ongoing peer-led programming to close the AI readiness gap, yielding key insights: human judgment remains essential, upfront planning determines success, and infrastructure matters as much as the model. This is a practical model for how enterprise design teams can organically adopt AI without top-down mandates.

items.title: Claude Code Can Now Browse the Web Without Opening Chrome
items.summary: Anthropic shipped a sandboxed in-app browser in Claude Code desktop, letting the agent read docs, inspect designs, and interact with websites — including local dev servers — without context-switching to an external browser. This closes a key gap for designers using Claude Code as a prototyping agent: it can now see the rendered output of what it builds.

items.title: Smart Cellular Bricks: Towards Collective Intelligence for the Physical World
items.summary: Sakana AI, IT University of Copenhagen, and Autodesk Research publish a Nature Communications paper describing roughly 200 circuit-board cubes ("Smart Cellular Bricks") that collectively classify 3D shapes using Neural Cellular Automata without centralized control. Each brick communicates only with its physical neighbors, yet the whole assembly converges on a correct shape label within ~3 minutes — and the system recovers autonomously from missing or faulty modules.

items.title: Taste Cannot Be Delegated
items.summary: Aurélie Radom argues that while AI handles prediction and pattern-matching, the judgment of which design outputs deserve to exist — "taste" — remains irreducibly human. As AI floods the market with generated interfaces and visuals, the designer's value shifts from producing outputs to exercising accountability for what gets made.

items.title: SIGGRAPH 2026 Keynotes: Tripo AI on Functional 3D Intelligence and NVIDIA on Visual Computing
items.summary: SIGGRAPH 2026 (July 19-23, Los Angeles) announces sponsored keynotes including Tripo AI presenting their shift from static 3D asset generation to functional, interactive 3D intelligence applicable to gaming, 3D printing, and robotics simulation. NVIDIA Research will cover the latest graphics and simulation breakthroughs, making this the week's key bellwether for AI+3D design tools.

items.title: ASSEMCAD: Production-Ready CAD Assembly Generation from Natural Language
items.summary: ASSEMCAD is an axiom-grounded framework that generates production-ready multi-part CAD assemblies from natural language descriptions, decomposing the task into structured assembly specification, on-demand component synthesis, and geometry-aware verification. Unlike prior work that outputs static geometry, it produces mechanically executable assemblies with valid joint structures and articulated mates, substantially improving physical validity over code-centric CAD generation baselines on AssemBench.

items.title: Cadence and Rapidus Partner on Agentic AI for Advanced SoC Design
items.summary: Cadence and Rapidus announced a collaboration integrating the Cadence InnoStack AI Super Agent into Rapidus' AI-Agentic Design Solution (Raads), targeting up to a 2X improvement in SoC design turnaround time for advanced-node 2nm semiconductors. The partnership deploys agentic AI at the physical implementation phase of chip design, combining Rapidus' AI-native foundry ecosystem with Cadence's EDA orchestration technology for design-manufacturing co-optimization.

items.title: Canva Code 2.0 just made vibe coding way less intimidating for everyone
items.summary: Canva Code 2.0 brings AI-powered app and website creation to all Canva users, combining natural language prompting with Canva's existing design tools to let designers build functional, on-brand digital experiences without coding expertise. It distinguishes itself from generic vibe-coding tools by integrating directly into the design workflow with real-time collaboration, letting designers customize layouts, branding, and content while the AI generates the underlying code.

items.title: Figma: Bring Code-backed Screens onto the Canvas with Variables Attached
items.summary: Figma now binds colors, type, and spacing tokens to existing file variables when code-backed screens arrive on the canvas via the Figma MCP server, Make previews, or the Chrome extension. This closes the design-to-code fidelity gap by letting teams refine real production layouts in Figma instead of rebuilding them from scratch.

items.title: Arko-T: A Foundation Model for Text-to-Structured 3D Generation
items.summary: Arko-T is a 4B-parameter foundation model that maps natural-language design intent directly into executable parametric CAD programs, preserving editable features, parameters, and construction logic rather than just generating renderable meshes. It uses execution-grounded supervision and formal design-state alignment throughout curation, normalization, and training to produce out-of-distribution mechanical parts coherently.

items.title: Embodied CAD: Solver-Grounded LLM Agents for Parametric B-Rep Assembly Modeling
items.summary: Embodied CAD introduces a solver-grounded LLM agent that iteratively selects actions from a stratified CAD skill library (L0-L4), resolves them into typed geometric operations, executes them in a CAD kernel, and uses solver feedback for GRPO-style reinforcement to produce valid, editable B-Rep assemblies. This approach closes the gap between LLMs writing plausible CAD scripts and actually passing the geometric kernel validation required for industrial use.

items.title: PR-CAD: Progressive Refinement for Unified Controllable and Faithful Text-to-CAD Generation with Large Language Models
items.summary: PR-CAD unifies text-to-CAD generation and editing in a single RL-enhanced agent that integrates intent understanding, parameter estimation, and precise edit localization, trained on a lifecycle-spanning dataset covering multiple CAD representations and both qualitative and quantitative descriptions. It achieves state-of-the-art controllability and faithfulness in both generation and refinement scenarios while significantly improving CAD modeling efficiency.

items.title: KDH-CAD: Knowledge-Data Hybrid CAD Learning under Data Scarcity
items.summary: KDH-CAD addresses the fundamental data scarcity problem in CAD AI by combining pretrained foundation model knowledge, structured domain knowledge from mechanical textbooks and tutorial videos, and small amounts of labeled CAD data—achieving 92.6% accuracy on real mechanical part classification with only 250 training samples. The framework shows that design textbooks and tutorials are valuable knowledge sources for grounding AI models in CAD domain concepts.

items.title: CADEvolve: Creating Realistic CAD via Program Evolution
items.summary: CADEvolve uses VLM-guided evolutionary program synthesis to grow CAD programs from simple primitives to industrial-grade complexity, producing an 8k dataset of complex parts and a 1.3M-script augmented corpus that exercises the full CadQuery operation set. A VLM fine-tuned on CADEvolve achieves state-of-the-art on Image2CAD tasks across DeepCAD, Fusion 360, and MCB benchmarks, showing that evolution-based data synthesis can overcome the complexity gap in public CAD corpora.

items.title: CADBench: A Multimodal Benchmark for AI-Assisted CAD Program Generation
items.summary: CADBench provides a unified evaluation framework of 18,000 samples spanning six dataset families and five input modalities (clean/noisy meshes, single/multi-view renders, photorealistic images), benchmarking 11 systems on geometric fidelity, executability, and program compactness. Key findings: specialized mesh-to-CAD models outperform code-generating VLMs under idealized conditions, but model rankings shift across metrics and modalities, revealing three systematic failure modes.

items.title: Seek-CAD: A Self-refined Generative Modeling for 3D Parametric CAD Using Local Inference via DeepSeek
items.summary: Seek-CAD is the first training-free system to incorporate both visual and Chain-of-Thought feedback in a self-refinement loop for parametric CAD generation, using locally deployed DeepSeek-R1 as the reasoning engine and a VLM to assess rendered step-by-step perspective images. Accepted at ICLR 2026, the work introduces an SSR (Sketch, Sketch-based feature, Refinements) CAD dataset and demonstrates that self-refinement with visual grounding substantially improves parametric model quality without fine-tuning.

items.title: Generative AI for Mechanical Engineering Design and Optimization (Nature Collection)
items.summary: Nature's curated research collection aggregates peer-reviewed work on how generative AI is enabling new modes of human-AI collaboration for mechanical engineering design—covering solution-space exploration, surrogate-model acceleration, and AI-driven design optimization across structural, thermal, and material domains. The collection serves as a living academic reference tracking the research frontier at the intersection of generative AI and engineering geometry.
